data.title: Nvidia rises after Morgan Stanley maintains Top Pick tag following DeepSeek impact
data.description:
Morgan Stanley reiterated Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) as its Top Pick, noting that DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) selloff is a buying opportunity.

Shares of Nvidia rose about 2% on Thursday.

Analysts led by Joseph Moore said that sentiment has worsened around potential longer term risks, but near term business continues to be firm, Blackwell supply visibility continues to build, and customer desire to spend is clearly on display.

The analysts acknowledge that DeepSeek creates some headwinds around exportcontrols and longer-term investment. They noted that it is a strong evolutionary upgrade in the AI space, but one of many that have taken place in the last year. In an area where Nvidia's CEO has said that they have improved AI performance at the processor level by one million x in the lastdecade and will do so again in the next decade, some deflation is not surprising, the analysts noted.

Moore and his team noted that confidence continues to build for both Hopper and Blackwell chips and they expect positive language to come back into the mix post quarter.

In addition, the analysts said that while investor sentiment around large training clusters is under pressure, there are signs that large clusters are still being built.

Inference seems likely to drive multiple years of growth, and Nvidia's position in inference remains strong, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that the stock market has favored Application Specific Integrated Circuits, or ASIC, over Graphic Processing Units, or GPUs, of late but they see that reversing in the second half of 2025. In the long term, both markets have significant promise, but the analysts' view is that there is now more room for relative sentiment to shift back in Nvidia's favor in the second half.

The analysts think the reason ASIC has outperformed post DeepSeek is based on agrowing emphasis on cost to serve, and a perception that ASIC is better equipped to bethat low cost inference platform at many of Nvidia's customers. However, the analysts added that it is Blackwell, not ASIC, shifting the cost curve in 2025.

data.title: Ladder Capital Q4 earnings miss as net interest income drops
data.description:
Ladder Capital (NYSE:LADR) Q4 earnings lagged the Wall Street consensus as net interest income fell more than expected. Its provision for bad debt, though, dramatically fell during the quarter.

Q4 distributable EPS of $0.27, missing the average analyst estimate of $0.28, fell from $0.30 in Q3 and $0.32 in Q4 2023.

Q4 net interest income of $27.2M, trailing the Visible Alpha consensus of $31.4M, declined from $38.4M in the previous quarter and $39.8M a year ago.

Undepreciated book value per share climbed to $13.88 at Dec. 31, 2024, from $13.81 at Sept. 30.

Provision for loan loss reserves of $47,000 vs. $3.1M in Q3 and $6.0M in Q4 2023.

Real estate operating income of $23.4M decreased from $25.3M in the prior quarter and increased from $23.1M a year ago.

Total costs and operating expenses dropped to $35.5M from $39.4M in Q3 and $36.2M in the year-earlier Q4.

Ladder Capital (NYSE:LADR) funded $129M of first mortgage loans in Q4.of advances on previously originated loans and received $M of balance sheet loan repayments.

It sold five net leased properties for $34M of net proceeds, generating $2.0M of aggregate gains, and one multifamily REO property for $11M of net proceeds, generating a $1.3M gain.

Cash and cash equivalents of $1.32B slipped from $1.61B at Sept. 30.

Ladder (LADR) stock rose 0.8% in Thursday morning trading.

Earlier, Ladder Capital non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 misses by $0.01, revenue of $68.61M beats by $2.34M

data.title: Matthews International gains after positive ruling in arbitration with Tesla
data.description:
Matthews International (NASDAQ:MATW) rose 6% after the company said it had a positive ruling in an ongoing business dispute with Tesla (TSLA).

An arbitrator ruled that Matthews (NASDAQ:MATW) can sell its Dry Battery Electrode technology solutions to customers other than Tesla, according to a statement on Thursday. Matthews intends to immediately resume marketing, selling and delivering its DBE products to other customers.

“This ruling confirms our rights in this groundbreaking technology and re-establishes what we have been saying for years – that we have valuable solutions to support the advancement of dry battery electrode technology,” Joseph C. Bartolacci, Matthews CEO said in the statement.

The arbitrator's ruling comes after Tesla (TSLA) filed its complaint against Matthews (MATW) in June.

data.title: Renesas expects automotive growth and 2.2% Q1 revenue increase amid cautious outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Renesas Electronics Corporation (OTCPK:RNECF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Hidetoshi Shibata stated that the business is stabilizing, with channel inventory reductions achieved in Q4. He noted that the company expects slight growth in Q1 2025, signaling the potential bottoming out of current conditions. However, uncertainties remain regarding the speed of recovery.

  • Shibata highlighted a change in revenue recognition standards for Altium, impacting overall revenue trends temporarily. This adjustment reflects the company's shift toward cloud services.

  • CFO Shuhei Shinkai reported Q4 revenue of JPY292.6 billion, gross margin of 54.9%, and operating profit of JPY75.4 billion. He attributed improvements in gross margin to cost reductions, including lower utility and logistics costs, and highlighted a better-than-expected operating margin of 25.8% due to cost control measures.

Outlook

  • Renesas projects Q1 2025 revenue at JPY309 billion, representing a 5.6% sequential increase. Gross margin is expected to be 54%, with an operating margin of 24%. Adjustments in Altium's revenue recognition are expected to temporarily reduce revenue by approximately 2%, but the company anticipates stable revenue growth thereafter.

  • Shibata indicated growth in both automotive and industrial infrastructure IoT (IIoT) segments in Q1, with device sales rising by 2.2% sequentially. He noted recovery in Europe and Japan, while cautioning about demand softness in China due to seasonal factors.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue reached JPY292.6 billion, exceeding the midpoint of guidance by 5.1%. Gross margin improved by 2.4 percentage points compared to forecasts, supported by production cost reductions and lower variable costs. Operating margin surpassed expectations by 3.3 percentage points.

  • The automotive segment experienced modest annual growth, while IIoT faced a revenue decline due to weaker market conditions. On a year-over-year basis, total revenue decreased by 8.2%, reflecting market challenges and currency impacts.

  • Inventory trends showed a Q4 increase in in-house inventory due to lower utilization rates. The company plans to slightly raise utilization in Q1 to support short-term lead time orders while maintaining lean inventory levels.

Q&A

  • Daiki Takayama, Goldman Sachs, inquired about Q1 revenue mix and the recovery timeline for automotive and non-auto segments. Shibata responded that automotive recovery in Europe is expected following significant Q4 inventory reductions. He noted that home appliances could see substantial growth in Q1, though this may not sustain at the same level in subsequent quarters.

  • Kenji Yasui, UBS Securities, asked about the outlook for regaining previous automotive revenue levels and market share in data centers. Shibata explained that automotive growth is expected to be gradual, with a focus on ADAS applications and DDR5 power management in cloud infrastructure starting Q3 2025.

  • Mikio Hirakawa, BofA Securities, questioned the impact of short-term lead time orders on production and shipment. Shinkai noted an increase in short-term orders but emphasized efforts to maintain flexibility through inventory management strategies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious sentiment, focusing on near-term revenue challenges and inventory adjustments. Concerns were raised about the sustainability of growth in specific segments like automotive and IIoT.

  • Management maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, emphasizing stabilization and strategic adjustments. Shibata acknowledged uncertainties but highlighted efforts to align operations with market realities.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected increased caution, particularly in addressing demand trends and inventory management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize cautious optimism, with a focus on stabilizing operations and aligning inventory with demand. This marked a departure from the more aggressive tone in the previous quarter.

  • Strategic priorities evolved to include adjustments in revenue recognition standards and targeted growth in automotive ADAS applications and cloud infrastructure.

  • Analysts maintained a skeptical tone, pressing for clarity on recovery timelines and growth drivers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified uncertainties in the speed of recovery, particularly in automotive and IIoT segments. Challenges in maintaining lean inventory levels and adapting to changing revenue recognition standards were also highlighted.

  • Analysts raised concerns about potential delays in demand recovery, particularly in China and Europe, and the impact of these factors on profitability.

Final Takeaway

Renesas Electronics Corporation's Q4 2024 earnings call reflected stabilization efforts amid challenging market conditions. While management highlighted gradual recovery in automotive and IIoT segments, analysts remained cautious about near-term prospects. The company’s focus on strategic adjustments, including changes to revenue recognition and targeted growth in ADAS and cloud infrastructure, positions it for potential long-term opportunities despite current uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ares Capital cut to Market Perform at Raymond James on valuation
data.description:
Raymond James on Thursday downgraded Ares Capital (NASDAQ:ARCC) shares to Market Perform from Outperform in what analyst Robert Dodd called a "valuation driven rationale, not a credit/portfolio issue."

"We continue to see an attractive risk/reward with the high quality and highly diversified portfolio, very robust current credit quality, and a dividend that can be covered even if base rates decline materially," Dodd wrote in a note.

However, he believes such factors have already been priced in with the current share-price premium.

The business development company's net asset value per share has risen 4.9% from the start of the credit cycle in Q4 2021, the analyst noted, trouncing the BDC group median decline of 7.3%.

Seeking Alpha's Quant rating system gives ARCC a valuation grade of A, with the best mark in price-to-earnings and the worst score in EV-to-sales.

The RayJay downgrade comes a day after Ares (NASDAQ:ARCC) turned in Q4 earnings that missed the Wall Street consensus estimate, as net investment income and interest income from investments also came in lower than analysts expected.

The lower interest income was likely due to a "combination of greater than expected spread compression and timing of deployments," Dodd wrote.

Dodd's Market Perform rate compares with the Quant rating of Hold, as well as the average SA analyst rating and the average sell-side analyst rating, both at Buy.

ARCC shares edged down 1% in Tursday morning trading.

data.title: Symbotic falls as Raymond James downgrades stock on disappointing earnings
data.description:
Symbotic (NASDAQ:SYM) shares fell more than 17% in Thursday trade after Raymond James downgraded its rating from Outperform to Market Perform following a disappointing first quarter results.

The Massachusetts-based warehouse automation company reported both top-line and bottom-line misses on Wednesday, and guided second quarter figures below analysts’ estimates.

Symbotic (SYM) reported first quarter GAAP EPS of -$0.03, on a revenue of $486.69M. For the second quarter of fiscal 2025, Symbotic expects revenue of $510 million to $530 million, vs. $533.30M consensus.

Besides the earnings miss, analysts at Raymond James cited flat backlog and a slowing pace of system deployments as some of the reasons for the downgrade.

Analysts said that SYM’s success in reducing time-to-deployment was a core tenant of their thesis, and the current pace of 24 months shows “little improvement from the rate achieved two years ago”.

“With gross margins expected in the mid-twenties, the company needs to comfortably double its deployed system over the next few years to maintain rapid revenue growth, generate material EBITDA to support its trading multiple, and be able to have capacity to sign meaningful new clients,” the analysts added.

data.title: Kellanova tops estimates as Mars' buyout of the company draws closer
data.description:
Kellanova (NYSE:K) edged 0.2% higher in early trading on Thursday after posting a mixed performance with its Q4 earnings report. The company's GAAP net sales decreased 2% year-on-year due to adverse foreign currency translation. However, organic net sales, which exclude currency impact, increased by 7%, driven by growth in both volume and price/mix.

Regional performance varied, with North America experiencing a 2% decrease in both reported and organic net sales. Despite this, the region's reported operating profit increased by 28%, while adjusted operating profit grew by 8% (9% excluding currency translation). In Europe, reported net sales decreased by 2%, with organic net sales declining by 1%. However, the region's reported operating profit increased by 24%, and adjusted operating profit rose by 32% (33% excluding currency).

Reported operating profit saw a significant increase of 62% compared to a year ago. The growth was attributed to favorable mark-to-market impacts, improved gross profit margin due to productivity and moderating supply chain costs, and reduced overhead. On an adjusted basis, excluding mark-to-market and one-time charges, operating profit increased by 14%, and by 20% when excluding currency translation.

EPS was reported at $0.92 vs. $0.83 consensus and $0.78 a year ago.

"Led by our strong emerging markets presence, we sustained better-than-expected top-line growth amidst challenging industry conditions, and we improved our profit margins faster than we had anticipated. We also embarked on an exciting next phase, as we prepare to combine with Mars," stated CEO Steve Cahillane.

Last year, Mars agreed to acquire Kellanova for $83.50 per share in cash, totaling $35.9 billion including assumed net leverage. The acquisition marks one of the largest transactions in the food industry in recent years, combining two iconic businesses with complementary categories, markets, and portfolios of beloved brands. The merger unites Mars, a global leader in pet care, snacking, and food, with Kellanova, a company known for its global snacking, international cereal and noodles. The acquisition is expected to close in the first half of this year.
